title: camara rugulosa
intro: Camara rugulosa, also known as the camara or wild camara, is a plant species in the Campanulaceae family, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have shown antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against infections. Camara rugulosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its herbal uses, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make ointments and creams for skin conditions.
